explanation: GetCart loads ALL CartItems into memory then filters by sessionId (full table scan), and then executes an N+1 query loop fetching each Product individually. Replace with a single server-side filtered query using .Where(c => c.SessionId == sessionId) and eager-load products via .Join or .Include to collapse N+1 round-trips into one query. This should significantly reduce p95 latency and increase RPS since cart retrieval is a high-frequency endpoint.

additionalOpportunities.description: OrdersController.GetOrder loads all OrderItems into memory then filters by orderId, plus N+1 product lookups ΓÇö same pattern as CartController.GetCart. Push filter to SQL and join products in a single query.
ReviewsController.GetReviewsByProduct and GetAverageRating both load all reviews into memory then filter by productId. Push Where/Average to the database query to avoid transferring unnecessary data.
CartController.ClearCart calls SaveChangesAsync inside a foreach loop causing one DB round-trip per item. Batch all removals with RemoveRange and call SaveChangesAsync once after the loop.
